heroTip: South Korea accepts credit cards almost everywhere — top up a T-Money Card for public transit.
bestCard: KTC or KBank Mastercard — competitive FX fees for KRW transactions.
topTips: Visa/Mastercard is accepted widely in Korea, including convenience stores and markets.
Buy a T-Money Card at the airport or subway station for the Seoul metro and buses.
Withdraw KRW from KEB Hana Bank or Shinhan Bank ATMs — both accept foreign cards.
Namdaemun / Dongdaemun markets prefer cash at smaller stalls — keep some on hand.
Claim 10% VAT refund at department store kiosks or the airport.
avoidTip: Never exchange KRW outside of Korea — rates are very poor. Withdraw or exchange in Korea directly.
dccNote: When paying by card in Korea, always select KRW. Avoid the home currency option if offered (DCC).

fxFeeItems.name: FX Fee (Foreign Transaction Fee)
fxFeeItems.detail: Fee your bank charges for foreign card use — typically 1.5%–2.5% of the transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: Spread
fxFeeItems.detail: The gap between mid-market rate and the rate your bank actually gives — usually 0.5%–2%.

fxFeeItems.name: ATM Fee
fxFeeItems.detail: Withdrawal fees from both your home bank and the local ATM — combined can reach $5–$10 per transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: DCC Markup
fxFeeItems.detail: Extra markup when you choose to pay in your home currency — often 3%–10% above the true market rate.

attractions.name: Gyeongbokgung Palace
attractions.detail: Seoul's grandest Joseon-dynasty palace — catch the royal changing-of-guard ceremony and rent a hanbok for free entry.
attractions.tip: Cards accepted at the main ticket booth. Nearby hanbok rental shops also take cards.

attractions.name: Bukchon Hanok Village
attractions.detail: Hillside neighbourhood of preserved traditional Korean houses with stunning views over Seoul — most scenic in the early morning.
attractions.tip: Teahouses and craft shops accept cards; street market stalls prefer cash KRW.

attractions.name: N Seoul Tower
attractions.detail: Iconic landmark on Namsan Mountain — famous love locks, panoramic Seoul views, and a revolving restaurant at the top.
attractions.tip: Cards and T-money card accepted for cable car and observatory entrance.

attractions.name: Jeju Island
attractions.detail: Volcanic island paradise with Hallasan National Park, Manjanggul lava tube caves, and black-sand beaches.
attractions.tip: Cards accepted widely on Jeju. Download Kakao Pay for quick payment at local restaurants.

attractions.name: Insadong
attractions.detail: Traditional arts district with craft galleries, tea houses, antiques, and the lively Ssamziegil courtyard marketplace.
attractions.tip: Carry cash KRW for market stalls and street food; restaurants and cafes accept cards.
